risks: Single-region deployment (eastus only) with no failover strategy — a regional outage would take down the entire solution
ACR adminUserEnabled is true — weaker security posture; Microsoft recommends disabling admin user and using managed identity or service principal
No managed identity or Key Vault resource — connection strings and secrets between services lack a centralized secure store
Cosmos DB Serverless is single-region only with a 5000 RU/request ceiling — not suitable for high-volume production without migration to provisioned throughput
Service Bus Basic tier lacks zone redundancy, geo-disaster recovery, topics/subscriptions, and duplicate detection
No Azure Communication Services (or Twilio) resource defined — Logic App SMS connector requires an external SMS provider that is not represented in the plan
All resources have public network access enabled with no private endpoints or VNet integration
Log Analytics workspace is present but no diagnostic settings are defined on other resources to route logs to it
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure Communication Services resource (Microsoft.Communication/communicationServices) to provide the SMS capability that Logic Apps references
Disable ACR admin user (adminUserEnabled: false) and configure managed identity or RBAC-based authentication for IoT Edge image pulls
Add an Azure Key Vault resource to store connection strings, Service Bus keys, and Cosmos DB credentials securely
Add explicit IoT Hub → ACR dependency since IoT Edge deployment manifests reference container images in ACR
Add diagnostic settings dependencies from IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, and Logic Apps to the Log Analytics workspace
Consider upgrading Service Bus to Standard tier if publish-subscribe fan-out or duplicate detection is needed in the future
Evaluate adding private endpoints for Cosmos DB, IoT Hub, and Service Bus for production network isolation
Add resource tags (environment, project, cost-center) across all resources for governance and cost tracking
